---
title: "About"
lang: en
---

## What this is

*White Ribbon* is an independent editorial project devoted to the quiet
mechanics of democratic decay and the equally quiet mechanics of its
prevention. We publish long-form essays in the tradition of Steven
Levitsky and Daniel Ziblatt's *How Democracies Die* — the tradition that
treats authoritarianism not as a sudden coup but as a slow erosion of
norms, an accumulation of small permissions.

## Where the name comes from

The white ribbon was the sign Belarusians wore in 2020. Regimes can ban
a flag, ban a colour-code, ban a slogan. They cannot ban the colour
white.

## What we publish

Essays. Long ones — usually 3,000 to 6,000 words. Footnoted, cited, in
the Chicago Author-Date style. Each piece is available in three languages:
English, Russian, Belarusian. The English version is canonical.

We do not publish news, hot takes, or institutional press. We do not
accept advertising. We have no editorial board, no funders, and no
political affiliation — only readers.

## Contact

Correspondence: editors at white-ribbon.net.
